creator: smh@FreeBSD.org
text:
I disagree the full message says:
---
Please keep in mind that the default location for my.cnf has changed
from "/var/db/mysql/my.cnf" to "/usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf".
Please merge your existing my.cnf with the new default and move
it to "/usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf".
If you do not want to move your my.cnf to the new location then
you must set "mysql_optfile" in /etc/rc.conf to "/var/db/mysql/my.cnf".
---

The problem is the the rc script actually use ${mysql_dbdir}/my.cnf and ${mysql_confdir}/my.cnf which are not always /var/db/mysql/my.cnf and /usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf respectively.

So while it is correct to say that the "default" location of my.cnf has moved from "/var/db/mysql/my.cnf" to "/usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf" the rest of the message is informing the user how to update which it needs to take into account the local values of mysql_dbdir and mysql_confdir otherwise the user will follow the instructions and it simply will not work.

I would suggest the following:
---
echo ""
echo "The default location for my.cnf has changed from \"%%MY_DBDIR%%/my.cnf\""
echo "to \"%%ETCDIR%%/my.cnf\"."
echo ""
echo "To prevent this message move \"${mysql_dbdir}/my.cnf\" to"
echo "\"${mysql_confdir}/my.cnf\"."
echo ""
echo "If you do not want to move your my.cnf to the new location then you"
echo "can either set \"mysql_optfile\" in /etc/rc.conf to"
echo "\"${mysql_dbdir}/my.cnf\" or symlink \"${mysql_confdir}/my.cnf\" to"
echo "\"${mysql_confdir}/my.cnf\"."
echo ""
---

Does that make sense?
